event: The Holy Family with Saint Catherine, Saint Sebastian, and a Donor
symbols: Saint Sebastian's arrows — the marks of martyrdom endured for faith
Saint Catherine with a book — learning and the defense of faith
Holy Family at center — the Incarnation as the axis of the sacred conversation
donor figure — the patron seeking intercessory connection with the sacred
description: A horizontal composition showing six figures in a sacra conversazione arrangement. At center-left, the Virgin Mary in a white veil holds the nude infant Jesus, who reaches across toward a female saint (Catherine) who leans in with a book. Behind Mary, an elderly Joseph is partially visible. At far right, the young Saint Sebastian stands bare-chested, arrows visible in his body, a white cloth wrapped around his hips. Another figure appears between Catherine and Sebastian. A landscape with a blue sky and distant hills is visible in the background.
theological_description: Sebastiano del Piombo's sacra conversazione — a 'holy conversation' — unites the Holy Family with post-biblical saints across time, visualizing the communion of saints. The infant Christ reaching toward Saint Catherine embodies the theological truth that all holiness flows from him: 'Apart from me you can do nothing' (John 15:5). Saint Sebastian, pierced by arrows yet standing, witnesses to the faithfulness described in Revelation 2:10: 'Be faithful, even to the point of death, and I will give you life as your victor's crown.' The Virgin Mary holding the child grounds the scene in the Incarnation — the moment when the eternal entered time (Galatians 4:4). Joseph's quiet presence continues his biblical role as the righteous guardian (Matthew 1:19). The sacra conversazione format itself expresses the belief that the Church transcends time: the saints of different centuries commune together in Christ, forming the 'one body' of 1 Corinthians 12:12-13.

scripture_refs.reason: Apart from me you can do nothing — all sanctity flowing from the Christ Child at center
God sent his Son, born of a woman — the Incarnation at the heart of the composition
Be faithful unto death — Sebastian's martyrdom witness
One body, many members — the communion of saints across centuries

_meta.title: The Holy Family with St. Catherine, St. Sebastian and a Donor
